what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML decision screen that compares fragmented project files, accounts, and channels with a centralised virtual hub, using runtime DNA organism visuals and injected HTML.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
Adds file-local styling for the page background, screen wrapper, and layered solution visual.
Uses DNA.org organism helpers to generate an artifact cluster, source panel visual, and interface fragment.
Builds the main screen with innerHTML from template strings.
Renders two comparison panels labelled Today and Recommended, separated by an arrow.
Includes visible buttons labelled Keep this and Choose this, with no handlers in this file.
standouts: The screen is structured as a side-by-side decision comparison.
The right-hand visual is composed by layering two organism-generated visuals inside a local .solvis frame.
The copy frames the decision around converting scattered files into one explorable space.
conventions: Uses dv-* class names for the decision-view layout.
Keeps most layout semantics in shared CSS while adding small file-local overrides.
Composes visual content through DNA.org runtime helpers.
Uses template-string HTML assembly rather than static body markup.
concerns: The action buttons have no file-local behavior attached.
The screen depends on global DNA.org runtime objects being available.
Several visual dimensions are hard-coded in pixels.
innerHTML assembly assumes generated visual strings are safe to inject.
notes: Brief this file as a self-contained artefact decision screen contrasting fragmented project materials with a centralised virtual hub.
questions: Should the Keep this and Choose this buttons trigger navigation or selection behavior elsewhere?
Are the fixed 300px and organism visual dimensions intentional for all phone layouts?
What guarantees that DNA.org organism helpers are loaded before this script runs?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated artefact-family decision-screen purpose matches the rendered comparison UI and its decision-focused copy.
intention_signals: It seems intended to present a clear before-and-after choice where scattered project files are contrasted with a single virtual hub.
novelty: The distinct element is the composed solution visual that layers a source panel and interface fragment as a compact representation of a virtual hub.
contribution: Contributes a decision-screen substrate with side-by-side comparison structure, organism-driven artefact visuals, concise decision copy, and a centralised-workspace framing that a fused system would keep as one available presentation pattern.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone HTML artefact-family decision screen renders a Today versus Recommended comparison between fragmented project files and a centralised virtual hub using DNA organism visuals.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for presenting a decision moment that encourages moving from scattered project materials into one explorable workspace.
